threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think ‘The Dolphin’ is just a pub name, not a ‘community centre’ for everyone’s dogs.
Heard a guy on the N35 last night bragging about how he dodged fare evasion cameras by wearing a hat. Thought it was a strange flex for 3am.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a guy in a pink tutu effortlessly cycling past a group of bemused tourists near Tempelhofer Feld.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you find a bar that serves beer brewed from recycled water. It’s a conversation starter, if nothing else!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone's from the Mission when they know the exact time the next bus is late because they've timed their coffee runs to match. No spreadsheets, just instinct.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo hop on the 19, holding a bouquet. The bus driver didn’t even blink. Just another Friday night in the city.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the Gaudí hype, but the real charm is in the small plaça bars where locals sip vermut and swap gossip.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You’ll see them still looking at maps on their phones, confused at the tram stops, while the locals just hop on and off like it’s second nature.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call the Overground the 'train' instead of 'the rail'. Locals only mention the bus when it's about the classic 55.
The 35 bus rolled in with a guy serenading the driver. By the time we hit Camberwell, they were having a full-on duet. Only in Brixton!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw a local artist painting a mural on the side of a kebab shop while an elderly woman picked up fallen flowers from the sidewalk.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you grab a döner at 3 AM and hear techno thumping from a nearby asphalt party. It’s like an unofficial rite of passage.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they instinctively check BART delays before even stepping out. Tourists just complain about the heat.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy singing opera on the 31 Balboa, totally transformed the commute. The whole bus felt like a Broadway audition.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can just walk into a bar and find tapas all day. Most locals eat lunch at 2, and dinner’s not until 9. It’s a different rhythm.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their baffled expressions when the trams don’t run on Sundays; locals just shrug and walk instead.
